document.title: Phase Characterization of TiO 2 Powder by XRD and TEM

figure.caption: Figure 1 Figure 1 X-ray diffraction of rutile TiO 2 (a) micropowders and (b) nanopowders.

analysis_paragraphs.text: XRD patterns of nano-TiO 2 and micro-TiO 2 in rutile and anatase phases are shown in Figure 1 and Figure 2 , respectively. In Figure 1 , XRD patterns exhibited strong diffraction peaks at 27°, 36° and 55° indicating TiO 2 in the rutile phase. On the other hand, in Figure 2 , XRD patterns exhibited strong diffraction peaks at 25°a nd 48° indicating TiO 2 in the anatase phase. All peaks are in good agreement with the standard spectrum (JCPDS no.: 88-1175 and 84-1286). From Figure 1 and Figure 2 , they were shown that the diffraction pattern peak intensity of the TiO 2 increases with increasing particles size. These results suggested that the nano-TiO 2 powder is composed of irregular polycrystalline. Amorphous revealed a broad pattern with low intensity; however, the effect of the amorphous materials on the broadening of the XRD patterns of nanosized TiO 2 is negligible.
analysis_paragraphs.relevant_text: XRD patterns of nano-TiO 2 and micro-TiO 2 in rutile and anatase phases are shown in Figure 1 and Figure 2 , respectively. In Figure 1 , XRD patterns exhibited strong diffraction peaks at 27°, 36° and 55° indicating TiO 2 in the rutile phase. From Figure 1 and Figure 2 , they were shown that the diffraction pattern peak intensity of the TiO 2 increases with increasing particles size.

combined_analysis_text:
XRD measurements were performed on the Bruker D8 Advance diffractometer operating in the reflection mode with Cu-Kα radiation (35 kV, 30 mA) and diffracted beam monochromator, using a step scan mode with the step of 0.075°( 2θ) and 4 s per step. Diffraction patterns of both anatase and rutile TiO 2 powders were compared with reference to JCPDS database. Additionally, the morphology of the powder was observed by TEM with a JEOL JEM-2100 ultra high resolution TEM operating at 160 kV.

XRD patterns of nano-TiO 2 and micro-TiO 2 in rutile and anatase phases are shown in Figure 1 and Figure 2 , respectively. In Figure 1 , XRD patterns exhibited strong diffraction peaks at 27°, 36° and 55° indicating TiO 2 in the rutile phase. From Figure 1 and Figure 2 , they were shown that the diffraction pattern peak intensity of the TiO 2 increases with increasing particles size.

document.title: Phase Characterization of TiO 2 Powder by XRD and TEM

figure.caption: Figure 3 Figure 3 Images of rutile phase. (a) TEM image of micro-TiO 2 powder; (b) TEM image of nano-TiO 2 powder; (c) SAED pattern of nano-TiO 2 powder and (d) HRTEM image of nano-TiO 2 powder.

analysis_paragraphs.text: TEM was used to further examine the particle size, crystallinity and morphology of samples. TEM bright field images of TiO 2 micropowders in rutile and anatase phases are shown in Figure (3a ) and (4a), respectively. It is clearly seen that the TiO 2 powders in rutile phase consist of both spherical and rod shapes but the particle of TiO 2 powders in anatase phase are mostly spherical morphology. Furthermore, it can be estimated that the particle size of samples in Figure TEM bright field images of TiO 2 nanopowders in rutile and anatase phases are shown in Figure (3b ) and ( 4b ), respectively. It can be estimated that the particle size of powders in Figure diffraction spots and rings of second phases, revealing their well crystalline. On the other hand, the SAED patterns of nano-TiO 2 powders in anatase phase (Figure (4c ) shows that the brightness and intensity of polymorphic ring is weak, so they are poorly crystallized and partly amorphous.
analysis_paragraphs.relevant_text: TEM bright field images of TiO 2 micropowders in rutile and anatase phases are shown in Figure (3a ) and (4a), respectively. Furthermore, it can be estimated that the particle size of samples in Figure TEM bright field images of TiO 2 nanopowders in rutile and anatase phases are shown in Figure (3b ) and ( 4b ), respectively.

analysis_paragraphs.text: The crystallinity of nano-TiO 2 powders can also be observed by phase-contrast images or Moire patterns. Figure (3d ) and (4d) show crystal lattice planes of nano-TiO 2 in rutile and anatase phases, respectively. It is seen that, for rutile phase, only one crystal lattice plane(110) with d-spacing of 0.301 nm is obtained, whereas those of anatase give many crystal lattice planes with d-spacing of 0.313 nm for the plane (101).
analysis_paragraphs.relevant_text: Figure (3d ) and (4d) show crystal lattice planes of nano-TiO 2 in rutile and anatase phases, respectively.

combined_analysis_text:
XRD measurements were performed on the Bruker D8 Advance diffractometer operating in the reflection mode with Cu-Kα radiation (35 kV, 30 mA) and diffracted beam monochromator, using a step scan mode with the step of 0.075°( 2θ) and 4 s per step. Diffraction patterns of both anatase and rutile TiO 2 powders were compared with reference to JCPDS database. Additionally, the morphology of the powder was observed by TEM with a JEOL JEM-2100 ultra high resolution TEM operating at 160 kV.

TEM bright field images of TiO 2 micropowders in rutile and anatase phases are shown in Figure (3a ) and (4a), respectively. Furthermore, it can be estimated that the particle size of samples in Figure TEM bright field images of TiO 2 nanopowders in rutile and anatase phases are shown in Figure (3b ) and ( 4b ), respectively.

Figure (3d ) and (4d) show crystal lattice planes of nano-TiO 2 in rutile and anatase phases, respectively.

On the other hand, the SAED patterns of nano-TiO 2 powders in anatase phase (Figure (4c ) shows that the brightness and intensity of polymorphic ring is weak, so they are poorly crystallized and partly amorphous.
